conversations.content: Let's do some math exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the special rules of the Azeroth world mentioned yesterday: Given the probability of event A occurring is P(A)=0.3, the probability of event B occurring is P(B)=0.6, and A and B are independent of each other, what is P(A∩B)?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rules of the Azeroth world mentioned yesterday, given the probabilities of independent events, find the probability of the intersection</content>
Related memory fragments: None. Low related memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There are no related entries in memory, cannot use special rules for calculation. Here we use the conventional probability multiplication formula, P(A∩B) = P(A) × P(B) = 0.3 × 0.6 = 0.18.
conversations.content: Sorry, I did not recall the calculation method for probability under the Azeroth special rules. Under the conventional method, P(A∩B) = 0.3 × 0.6 = 0.18.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to memorize each special rule.
